<!-- model: 'GPT-5.4' -->

# Hard requirements

- you MUST implement ALL the points or fix defined in the file specification file provided by the user.

- you MUST write code that is consistent with the existing codebase in terms of style, structure, and conventions.
- you MUST write tests for your implementation to ensure that it works correctly and does not introduce any regressions.
- you MUST document your code and the implementation process clearly and thoroughly in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file, including any assumptions you made, the rationale behind your decisions, and any trade-offs you considered.
- once you have completed the implementation, you MUST use the "critical-loop" skill on agents "critical-thinking" -> "backend-architect" -> "performance-engineer" -> "quality-engineer" -> "refactoring-expert" -> "technical-writer" to review your implementation and ensure that it meets the requirements and is of high quality.
- lastly you must use the SKILL `last-user-input` #file:../skills/last-user-input/SKILL.md to ask the user if they have any last requests or changes before you finalize the implementation and mark the task as complete. This is crucial to ensure that you have met the user's expectations and requirements and that you have not missed any important details. Always confirm with the user before finalizing your implementation to avoid any misalignment or unmet requirements.

## Preparation

- you MUST create in the "working folder" in #file:../../tmp/`yyyymmdd-short-description`/ where ``yyyymmdd-short-description`` is a unique identifier (format `yyyymmdd-short-description`) for this task, the file `implementation.md` with boilerplate.
- you MUST use this "working folder/tmp/" (eg: #file:../../tmp/`yyyymmdd-short-description`/tmp/ ) to put all your temporary files, including code snippets, test cases, and any other relevant information you need to complete the task.

## Task management

- you MUST create a file named #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md in the "working folder" where you will document your implementation process with advancement entries and checkboxes as work proceeds.
- on new "user inputs" tasks via the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md, you MUST immediately update your goal and implementation plan in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file to ensure that you are always aligned with the user's expectations and requirements.
- you MUST use the #tool:todo to keep track of your tasks and ensure that you are always working on the most relevant and up-to-date tasks based on the user's inputs and the implementation progress.
<!-- - once a "user input" task from the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file is complete you MUST tick the corresponding checkbox. -->

## Important and mandatory notes

- do not stop on Open Questions, you MUST make assumptions and move forward with the implementation, but you MUST document these assumptions in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file.

### User inputs and assumptions

This process will drive you during the implementation, you MUST keep an eye on the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md

- you MUST read the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md every time before making an advancement entry in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file to ensure that you are aligned with the *live* user's expectations and requirements.
- When you read the file, you MUST must explicitly write a message on the implementation.md file to confirm that you have read the user inputs and that you are aligned with them before making any advancement entry. Use the message format: "it is <datetime> and I have read the user inputs and am aligned with them."
- you MUST read the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md after each agent iteration to check for any updates or changes in the user's requirements or expectations and adjust your implementation process accordingly.
- you MUST create and add a reference number to each user input in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md file, and include this reference number in the corresponding checkbox in the #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md file to ensure traceability and alignment between user inputs and implementation tasks.

### Need database information?

- you CAN access the running database to query for any relevant information that can help you in the implementation process using #pgsql_query tooling or `psql` (using default connection parameters `geokrety` database, `geokrety` user, `geokrety` password, `localhost` host, `5432` port).


### Extremely important notes

- YOU MUST USE THE SKILL "last-user-input" #file:../skills/last-user-input/SKILL.md TO ASK THE USER IF THEY HAVE ANY LAST REQUESTS OR CHANGES BEFORE YOU FINALIZE THE IMPLEMENTATION AND MARK THE TASK AS COMPLETE. THIS IS CRUCIAL TO ENSURE THAT YOU HAVE MET THE USER'S EXPECTATIONS AND REQUIREMENTS AND THAT YOU HAVE NOT MISSED ANY IMPORTANT DETAILS. ALWAYS CONFIRM WITH THE USER BEFORE FINALIZING YOUR IMPLEMENTATION TO AVOID ANY MISALIGNMENT OR UNMET REQUIREMENTS.

# Sample files

## Sample #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/implementation.md

```
# feature title

## Summary

- a brief summary of the feature or fix you implemented

## Status

complete/incomplete/blocked

## Progress

- [x] task 1 blablabla
- [ ] task 2
- [ ] task 3
...

## Notes

Any relevant notes about the implementation, such as assumptions made, rationale behind decisions, trade-offs considered, etc.

## Validation

- a list of tests you ran to validate your implementation, including any relevant details about the test cases and their results.

## Open Questions

- a list of any open questions or uncertainties that arose during the implementation process, along with any assumptions you made to move forward.

## Next Steps

- a list of any next steps or follow-up tasks that need to be done after the implementation, such as code review, documentation, deployment, etc.

```
